description: Connect the messaging server to external systems through registered bridges. Services receive a filtered event stream, provision virtual users/rooms, and respond to existence queries.

actors.name: Application Service
actors.description: Registered external program that consumes and produces room events

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server routing events to registered services and enforcing namespace rules

actors.name: User
actors.description: User whose events may be routed to application services

fields.name: service_id
fields.label: Unique identifier for the registered application service

fields.name: as_token
fields.label: Shared secret the service presents to authenticate inbound requests

fields.name: hs_token
fields.label: Shared secret the homeserver presents when calling the service

fields.name: user_namespace_regex
fields.label: Regular expression matching user IDs managed by this service

fields.name: room_alias_regex
fields.label: Regular expression matching room aliases managed by this service

fields.name: exclusive
fields.label: Whether the namespace is exclusively owned by this service

fields.name: transaction_id
fields.label: Identifier for an event batch delivered to the service

fields.name: last_processed_position
fields.label: Stream position tracking how far the service has received events

values.description: No pending events; service is up to date
Event batch is being sent to the service
Delivery failed; waiting for backoff before retry
Service is consistently unreachable; events are queued

transitions.description: New events arrive that match the service's interest patterns
Service returns an error or does not respond

namespaces: Application services register interest using regular expressions for user IDs, room aliases, and room IDs
Exclusive namespaces prevent other users and services from creating users or aliases in that pattern
delivery: Events are delivered in strictly sequential order per service; new batch not sent until previous acknowledged
Before delivering an event, the homeserver queries the service to check if an unknown user should be provisioned
A maximum of 100 events are batched per transaction
Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff; last processed position persisted for recovery
Ephemeral events (typing, presence, read receipts) are delivered separately from room events
Services may act as users in their namespace by authenticating with their service token

given: one or more room events match the service's interest patterns

then.description: Event batch sent as a transaction to the service's endpoint

events_delivered.result: Service receives and processes the events

given: homeserver encounters an unknown user ID
user ID matches the service's user namespace

then.description: Homeserver queries the service's user existence endpoint

user_provisioned.result: Service creates the virtual user; subsequent operations proceed

given: homeserver encounters an unknown room alias
alias matches the service's alias namespace

then.description: Homeserver queries the service's alias existence endpoint

alias_provisioned.result: Service creates the room for the alias; lookup resolves successfully

given: service endpoint returns an error or times out
delivery_failed.result: Delivery retried with backoff; event stream paused for this service

given: user or alias creation targets an exclusive namespace owned by another service

errors.message: This alias or user ID is reserved by a registered application service

events.name: appservice.events_delivered
events.description: A batch of room events was successfully delivered to a service

events.name: appservice.user_provisioned
events.description: A service provisioned a virtual user in response to a query

events.name: appservice.alias_provisioned
events.description: A service provisioned a room in response to an alias query

events.name: appservice.delivery_failed
events.description: Event delivery to a service failed; retry scheduled

related.reason: Application services enforce exclusive namespace rules on alias creation
Services can create rooms on behalf of users in their namespace
Services may bridge federated rooms from external networks

goals.description: Connect the messaging server to external systems through registered bridges. Services receive a filtered event stream, provision virtual users/rooms, and respond to existence queries.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable
Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
